channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com

item.title: NYT Strands #834 Hints and Answers for Today, June 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #834 for Monday, June 15, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 15, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #834 are all words related to overcoming obstacles.

General Strands Hints for June 15

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Word that means to best your struggles.
  • Hint 2: Another word for defeat.
  • Hint 3: Divide & ___

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #834 for June 15, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 15, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • QUIT
  • SHARE
  • MORE
  • MOUNTS
  • MOUNT

Today’s Strands Answer for Game #834 on June 15, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • CONQUER
  • SURMOUNT
  • OVERCOME
  • VANQUISH
  • QUASH
  • SPANAGRAM: YOUVEGOTTHIS

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #834?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 16 will be Strands Game #835

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Complete Halo: Campaign Evolved Trophy List—How To Get All Trophies & Achievements
item.content:encoded:
The Halo: Campaign Evolved trophy list is a trip down memory lane. If you’re excited to see one of the most iconic shooters in the modern day, then check out the complete trophy list first.

It’s easy to forget that the original Halo is getting a full-blown remake in 2026. There are so many elite games this year, but Halo will be a day one for so many. Why wouldn’t it be? It’s a classic!

Well, well ahead of time, we’ve learned about the complete Halo: Campaign Evolved Trophy List.

How Many Achievements & Trophies In Halo: Campaign Evolved?

There’s a grand total of 58 PlayStation Trophies and Xbox Achievements for Halo: Campaign Evolved.

The list itself will NOT be light work. Much of this will be familiar to you, old-school Xbox players who played it back in the day or via the Master Chief Collection.

  • More Insider Gaming: Halo: Campaign Evolved Release Date Revealed

Complete the game on Legendary, find all the Skulls, and do special actions and challenges in specific missions.

You’ve got this, Spartan.

All Halo: Campaign Evolved Trophies & Achievements

Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord if you have every intention of completing the Halo: Campaign Evolved list!

Check out Halo: Campaign Evolved Pre-Order Bonuses for more on the game.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Xbox To Close South of Midnight Developer Compulsion Games
item.content:encoded:
An award-winning studio is facing closure. Insider Gaming sources can confirm Xbox is planning to close South of Midnight developer Compulsion Games.

News of the pending closure was first reported by Ethan Gach and Rebekah Valentine at Kotaku.

Speculation of the studio’s future began to pick up late last week and over the weekend. In that time, multiple sources, including high-level employees at Compulsion, spoke to Insider Gaming under the condition of anonymity.

UPDATE: According to Kotaku’s Valentine, “Compulsion leadership is in ‘negotiations’ with Microsoft over the studio’s fate, but the details of these negotiations have not been disclosed.”

Despite work being in progress on the next game at the studio, employees say that there has been “worry” and “fear” for months. After Phil Spencer stepped down earlier this year, that fear grew as many said they felt changes were going to come down under new leadership.

“It didn’t matter who took over,” one source said. “We all talked about what it meant for us. No one was confident we’d be ok.”

Another high-level employee said that the messaging coming into the studio didn’t do anything to calm nerves.

“Nothing was definitively said [about Compulsion being in trouble], but there was nothing said to assure us that things were okay,” they said. “We were just left hanging when the topic would be brought up. Just feels like a matter of time.”

Released in April 2025, South of Midnight was received positively by critics, even winning a Peabody Award in the Immersive and Interactive category. Commercially, however, the game failed to meet internal expectations at Xbox.

The Montreal-based studio currently employs around 90-100 people. It was acquired by Xbox in 2018.

Xbox did not respond to Insider Gaming’s request for comment by the time of publication.

In other news, read about the struggles surrounding the development of the next Ghost Recon game at Ubisoft. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

item.title: Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports—Release, Platforms, Multiplayer, File Sizes & Remaster Status
item.content:encoded:
Get ready for a taste of history as Black Ops 1 & 2 ports are happening. The classic Treyarch shooters will be released for a new generation, but there’s some confusion about what they offer. This guide looks into everything you need to know about Black Ops 1 & 2 ports.

Call of Duty: Black Ops 1 and Black Ops 2 were unexpectedly rated in Korea. It was curious timing, given that we’d only just learned about Modern Warfare 4 officially. Regardless, Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports are ready to go into the firing line.

When Are Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports Coming Out?

One of the few key details that isn’t known is when the Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports are releasing, but the anticipation is that it could be a shadow drop very soon, and they need to be available sooner rather than later, so as not to clash with Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4, or other big debuts.

The tail end of 2026 is being dictated by GTA 6. It’s more inevitable than Thanos, and as such, many games, including the year’s biggest AAA releases, are launching well before November.

  • More Insider Gaming: New Modern Warfare 4 Map is ‘Crazy’ and Has Over 500 Possible Combinations

Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 is coming at the end of October, and there’s a lot between now and then. The most sense for two rereleased Call of Duty games would surely need to be in the quieter summer months.

Again, this is all speculation, but if they are announced soon, you’ll know here first.

Are Call of Duty: Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports Remasters?

No, these are marginally upgraded versions of the original files, and they are not considered fully fledged remasters in the same way the Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare and Modern Warfare 2 campaign remasters were.

The line here is quite blurred, as the ports allegedly do have improvements; we can thank datamining for this information.

RemasterRadar has said:

‘This isn’t a lazy 1:1 copy, and it isn’t Xbox-style backwards compatibility (running the old build as-is). Real work went in: new languages that weren’t in the originals, plus PS5-enhanced flags baked into the build. It can’t run on legacy PS3 servers either, so it relies on modern PSN online infrastructure. Not a full remake, but clearly an enhanced, worked-on port.’

We’ll have to wait and see the full extent of the ports, but it’s expected they won’t be marketed as proper remasters.

Will Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports Have Multiplayer?

More datamines suggest that both Black Ops 1 and Black Ops 2 will include a multiplayer component.

Referring to RemasterRadar and other accounts, it’s been discovered that ‘joinSession’ exists in the files.

This would only be present if you could join other players in matchmaking, etc. It’s in the files, but it doesn’t necessarily mean the final ports will include multiplayer.

Unfortunately, if they do have multiplayer, it seems that all data will be wiped, meaning even if you’ve dumped hundreds of hours into Black Ops 1 or 2 on your account, you must start all over again with a clean slate.

Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports File Sizes

Multiple sources have indicated that Black Ops 1 and 2 ports will be in the region of 9 GB to 30 GB.

Here’s the latest on Black Ops file sizes in different regions.

All Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports Platforms

Data suggests the Black Ops ports will be available to buy on PS4 and PS5, and while it’s not certain, there’s also a good chance they’re coming to Xbox and PC, with Switch 2 versions possible as well.

There’s no reason why these would only be on PlayStation; it makes no sense. I would definitely say we’ll see the ports on Xbox One, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.

Also, Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 is coming to the Nintendo Switch 2, and it feels like a door has opened.

  • More Insider Gaming: Call of Duty Promises No BS or Silly Skins in Modern Warfare 4

Let’s go back in time 14 years and check out the original Call of Duty: Black Ops 2 reveal trailer!

Once they’re out, will you be buying either Black Ops 1 or Black Ops 2? Or is it a case of saying goodbye to the bank account and just buying both? Why not discuss the games, or CoD in general, via our Insider Gaming Discord?

Check out all Modern Warfare 4 consoles and platforms for more content.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/black-ops-1-2-ports/

item.title: TheShy Returns to Competitive LoL and Invictus Gaming
item.content:encoded:
Legendary toplaner Kang ‘TheShy’ Seung-lok has returned to Invictus Gaming (IG) and competitive play ahead of LPL Split 3.

The former League of Legends World Champion has sat out most of the 2026 season, having announced he was taking a break back in December.

TheShy has taken back his place as IG’s starting LPL toplaner, replacing Chen ‘Breathe’ Chen who has now become a free agent.

Substitute toplaner Lim ‘Soboro’ Seong-min and Zou ‘Nia’ Guang-Lu, who shared the mid lane role last split, have also both left the organisation.

TheShy Returns to Rescue IG

IG will be hoping the return of TheShy will help change the team’s fortunes after struggling all year.

They finished 11-12 in the LPL Playoffs last split, being swept 3-0 by ThunderTalk Gaming in the Play-In.

Song ‘Rookie’ Eui-jin, who won LoL Worlds 2018 with TheShy, is still on the IG roster despite sitting out Split 2 and will likely return to the line-up too.

This means the IG roster for LPL Split 3 will be:

  • Top – TheShy
  • Jungle – Wei
  • Mid – Rookie and Renard
  • Bot – JiaQi
  • Support – Meiko

Allowing veteran players to take a rest could be a smart move, as it worked for Top Esports (TES).

TES brought back Gao ‘Tian’ Tian-Liang and Yu ‘JackeyLove’ Wen-Bo for Spring, and the former World Champions helped the team qualify for MSI.

IG will be hoping to do something similar on the road to LoL Worlds 2026.

Last year, IG and TheShy qualified for Worlds but didn’t get to the Main Event after losing to eventual winners T1 in the Play-In.

If BLG continue their fine form, the LPL will have a fourth seed at Worlds and IG will have a huge opportunity to go further than last year.

Especially as the chaotic LPL Playoffs with huge upsets by Team WE proved the league is wide open.

Can TheShy help IG turn their fortunes around? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News
